items.title: Factory hits a $1.5B valuation to build AI coding tools for enterprises
items.summary: Factory raised a new round at a $1.5 billion valuation as it pushes AI coding systems built for large enterprise software teams.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: enterprise coding demand is still strong for AI products that promise governance, deployment control, and team-scale automation instead of solo developer tooling alone.

items.title: Luma launches an AI-powered production studio with Wonder Project
items.summary: Luma introduced a production studio partnership with Wonder Project that packages its video-generation stack for film and media workflows.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: generative video vendors are moving up the stack from model demos toward workflow products tied to real entertainment production teams.

items.title: Upscale AI is reportedly raising at a $2B valuation for global AI IT services
items.summary: Upscale AI is in talks to raise at a roughly $2 billion valuation as investors back AI-first IT and services delivery for global customers.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: services firms built around AI-native delivery are becoming a parallel bet to pure model providers and infrastructure startups.

items.title: Physical Intelligence says its new robot brain can solve tasks it was never taught
items.summary: Physical Intelligence unveiled a new robotics foundation model it says can generalize to tasks and situations that were not part of training.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: robotics vendors are increasingly framing model progress as general-purpose behavior transfer rather than narrow task automation.

items.title: OpenAI expands Codex with deeper desktop control to counter Anthropic
items.summary: OpenAI rolled out a beefed-up Codex experience that can take more direct control over desktop workflows as coding-agent competition intensifies.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: coding copilots are competing on how much real work they can execute locally, not just on model quality inside a chat box.

items.title: Anthropic's product chief leaves Figma's board amid reports of a competing product
items.summary: Anthropic chief product officer Mike Krieger left Figma's board after reports that he plans to launch a competing design product.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: the boundary between AI assistants and design software keeps collapsing as leading model vendors target full creative workflows.

items.title: Google now lets users browse the web side-by-side with AI Mode
items.summary: Google updated AI Mode so people can keep web results visible alongside the conversational panel instead of switching between views.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: search products are converging on hybrid interfaces that preserve links and sources while still centering AI-generated answers.

items.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to show companies where AI agents break
items.summary: InsightFinder raised $15 million to help teams trace, diagnose, and fix failures across multi-step AI agent systems.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: observability is becoming core AI infrastructure as production agents create longer chains of hidden failure modes.

items.title: Roblox adds agentic AI tools to plan, build, and test games
items.summary: Roblox expanded its assistant with agentic tooling that can help creators scope, assemble, and validate game experiences.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: consumer creator platforms are adopting autonomous AI workflows, not just prompt boxes, to increase publishing speed.

items.title: Google adds Nano Banana image generation to Gemini and expands Personal Intelligence
items.summary: Google brought Nano Banana-powered image generation into Gemini and widened access to its Personal Intelligence features in the U.S.
items.excerpt: Why it matters: Google is bundling multimodal generation and personalization into Gemini to make the assistant more useful beyond pure chat.
